The prior log‚Äôs live thread is the Turn 9‚Äì27 dynamic tame-inertia candidate; the earlier scalar and fixed-œÑ‚àí2 candidates are not just unproved but explicitly refuted by finite-tame or index-two tests in the prior record.The surrounding literature check is consistent with that status: the known Jannsen‚ÄìWingberg-style local presentation theory is tied to Demu≈°kin kernels over tame subextensions, Jarden‚ÄìShusterman discuss finite presentability of local absolute Galois groups, and Schneider‚Äôs dyadic work/conjectural setting is exactly where the Q2 case remains delicate.Numdam+2 Tel Aviv University Math Department+2

The conclusion was right, but the displayed algebra was not. Here is the corrected calculation.

Fix a finite tame quotientŒîe,f=‚ü®œÉÀâ,œÑÀâ‚à£œÑÀâe=1,œÉÀâf=1,œÉÀâœÑÀâœÉÀâ‚àí1=œÑÀâ2‚ü©,e¬†odd,¬†e‚à£2f‚àí1.

Let Ue,f be the candidate‚Äôs pro-2 kernel above Œîe,f, and letŒ∫(Xg)=Œ∫(Yg)=1,Œ∫(S)=0 be the sign/orientation character already isolated in the prior turns. Put Ve,f=kerŒ∫. Work over the unramified coefficient ring O containing an e-th root Œ±ÓÄ†=1, and decompose the Ve,fab matrix into œÑ-character blocks. Let t be the deck involution of the sign cover.

For a norm wordNa (u)=(uœÑa)œÄ,the Fox coefficient on the œÑ-character Œ± block is not 1‚àít 1‚àítŒ±a.

The correct completed geometric series isDa=j=0‚àëœÄ‚àí1(tŒ±a)j=1‚àítŒ±a 1‚àí(tŒ±a)œÄ.

Because œÄ‚â°1(mod2) and œÄ‚â°0(mode), we have(tŒ±a)œÄ=tœÄŒ±aœÄ=t.

HenceDa=1‚àítŒ±a1‚àít.

Now 1‚àítŒ±a is a unit in the local ring O[C2], since its reduction has scalar part 1‚àíŒ±ÀâaÓÄ†=0. ThusDa‚àà(1‚àít)O[C2],the radical of the sign-cover group algebra. Therefore the coefficient contributed by the correcting factorx‚àí1(xœÑ‚àíŒ∑)œÄ is a unit: it is ‚Äú‚àí1 plus a radical term,‚Äù up to harmless sheet conventions. Consequently every nontrivial odd tame-inertia character block has only unit Smith pivots. This makes precise the assertion in Turn 26 that the nontrivial œÑ-blocks cannot contribute C2,C4,C8,‚Ä¶-torsion.

So all 2-primary torsion in Ve,fab is concentrated in the œÑ-invariant block. That block is exactly the unramified calculation from Turn 25. Iff=2af0,f0odd,and s‚â°œÄ(modf),s‚â°1(mod2a),s‚â°0(modf0),then in the pro-2 kernelœÉœÄ=œÉsSq,q=f1‚àís‚ààZ2.

The sign-kernel Smith factor in the invariant block is 2(fq+s+1).

For the candidate‚Äôs q=(1‚àís)/f, this becomes 2((1‚àís)+s+1)=4.Thus the Turn 26 conclusion is now on firmer algebraic footing:Ve,fab‚âÖZ22ef+1‚äïC4 for every legal finite tame quotient (e,f). The important repair is that the nontrivial tame-character unit-pivot proof usesDa=1‚àítŒ±a1‚àít,not its reciprocal.This also makes Turn 27‚Äôs next-cyclotomic enumeration essentially formal. Once the prior finite-tame cup-product computations are accepted, Ue,f is a Demu≈°kin group with q=2, and Ve,f is an index-two open Demu≈°kin subgroup with q=4. Demu≈°kin groups are classified by rank together with the dyadic orientation/torsion invariant, and local maximal pro-p Galois groups are the standard arithmetic examples of these groups.Cambridge University Press & Assessment+1

Let œáV:Ve,f‚ÜíZ2√óbe the orientation character. Since q(Ve,f)=4,œáV(Ve,f)‚äÇ1+4Z2butœáV(Ve,f)ÓÄ†‚äÇ1+8Z2.

ThereforeŒª=4œáV‚àí1mod2 is a nonzero character in H1(Ve,f,F2). For an index-two subgroup W=kerœà‚äÇVe,f,q(W)=8 if and only if Œª‚à£W=0, which happens if and only if œà=Œª. All other index-two subgroups still have q=4.

Sinced(Ve,f)=2ef+2,there are 2 2ef+2‚àí1 index-two subgroups of Ve,f. Hence the uniform distribution should be

#¬†subgroups2 2ef+2‚àí21Wab free¬†rank 4ef+14ef+1 2-primary¬†torsionC4C8.

For (e,f)=(3,2), this gives 214‚àí2=16382 subgroups with C4 and one with C8, exactly Turn 27‚Äôs computation. The new point is that this is not a special ramified computation; it follows uniformly from the corrected Ve,fab‚âÖZ22ef+1‚äïC4 calculation plus Demu≈°kin orientation theory.This still does not prove the absolute Galois presentation. It proves a substantial cyclotomic consistency layer for the live word, and it fixes a real algebraic slip in the previous proof of that layer. The decisive missing step remains the dyadic formation/rigidity theorem: one must show that the candidate‚Äôs full filtered (œÉ,œÑ)-action on all finite tame Demu≈°kin kernels is the actual arithmetic action, not merely that the kernels and their first cyclotomic layers have the correct abstract invariants.

ESTABLISHED: The live dynamic candidate remains unrefuted. I corrected the nontrivial tame-character sign-kernel Fox coefficient toDa=(1‚àít)/(1‚àítŒ±a),which still proves unit Smith pivots on every nontrivial odd tame-inertia block. Therefore the only sign-kernel torsion comes from the œÑ-invariant unramified block, giving ker(Œ∫)ab‚âÖZ22ef+1‚äïC4 for all finite tame (e,f). As a formal Demu≈°kin consequence, the Turn 27 C4/C8 index-two distribution holds uniformly: 2 2ef+2‚àí2 subgroups with C4, one with C8.

GAP: This is still not a full presentation proof. The finite-tame Demu≈°kin kernels, sign kernel, and first two cyclotomic layers now look correct, but the full dyadic formation/rigidity step is still missing: the exact filtered tame action selected by the relation must be identified with arithmetic Frobenius/tame inertia over every finite tame subextension.

NEXT: Stop doing isolated Smith tests and formulate the missing rigidity statement precisely: an oriented Demu≈°kin kernel with the candidate‚Äôs exact œÉœÄ=œÉsS (1‚àís)/f and œÑ‚àí2œÄ operator data should be shown uniquely equivalent to the arithmetic finite-tame Galois formation.[BREAKTHROUGH]
